lesson.menu_desc: Future Forms — going to, will, present continuous & Planning — 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 — Making Plans and Talking About the Future
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Vanessa Giuriati | Aula 5 — Making Plans and the Future

listenings.text: Let me tell you about my plans for next week. It is going to be a busy one. On Monday, I am meeting my team in the morning to prepare for a big presentation. On Tuesday, I am going to finish the report, because the deadline is Wednesday. I am a little nervous, but I think it will go well. On Thursday, I have a doctor's appointment in the afternoon, so I am going to work from home that day. And on Friday, I am not going to work at all. I am taking the day off. My sister and I are going to drive to the coast for the weekend. I am really looking forward to it. I think it is going to be exactly what I need.

People often ask me about my plans for the future, and I always give the same answer. I am going to keep things simple. Next year, I am going to learn to cook properly. I am not very good now, but I will practice every weekend. In two or three years, I am going to travel more. I have a small list of places I want to see. And one day, when I am older, I am going to live near the sea. That is my real dream. I do not know the exact date yet, but I am sure it will happen. A plan does not need to be perfect. It just needs a first step. So this year, my first step is to save a little money every month.

extra_audio.text: First, I am going to confirm the meeting. Then, I am going to prepare the report. After that, I will send it to my manager. On Friday, I am attending a workshop. At the end, I am going to take the weekend off.
